questions.question: Where does the logical Z operator (Z_L) connect in a planar surface code?
questions.explanation: The logical Z operator Z_L connects rough boundaries (typically top to bottom). It consists of Z operators on all qubits along any vertical column. Different columns give equivalent operators.

questions.question: Why do logical X_L and Z_L anticommute?

options.text: They share exactly one qubit (at their intersection)
They connect to the same boundaries

questions.explanation: X_L (horizontal) and Z_L (vertical) cross at exactly one qubit. Since X and Z anticommute on a single qubit, and commute everywhere else, the total effect is anticommutation: X_L Z_L = -Z_L X_L.

questions.question: What is the dimension of the code space for a planar surface code?
questions.explanation: The code space has dimension 2, encoding exactly one logical qubit. This comes from d² data qubits minus (d²-1) independent stabilizer constraints, leaving 2^1 = 2 dimensions.

questions.question: How is the logical |0_L⟩ state typically prepared?

options.text: Initialize all qubits to |0⟩, measure X-stabilizers, apply corrections
Initialize all qubits to |+⟩, measure all stabilizers
Apply X_L to the vacuum state

questions.explanation: To prepare |0_L⟩: initialize all data qubits to |0⟩ (satisfies Z-stabilizers automatically), measure X-stabilizers to project into code space, then apply X_L if the Z_L measurement gives -1 to ensure we have the +1 eigenstate.

questions.question: Two Z_L operators on different columns are related by:

options.text: Their product is a product of Z-stabilizers
One is the inverse of the other

questions.explanation: Any two Z_L operators on different columns differ by a product of Z-stabilizers (the plaquettes between the columns). This makes them equivalent as logical operators—they have the same effect on the logical qubit.

questions.question: What happens in a destructive Z_L measurement?

options.text: Only the qubits along Z_L are measured
All data qubits are measured in Z basis, then parity is computed
The code remains intact for further computation

questions.explanation: In destructive Z_L measurement, all data qubits are measured in the Z basis. The Z_L eigenvalue is the product (parity) of outcomes along any Z_L path. This destroys the code state but gives the logical measurement result.

questions.question: Transversal CNOT between two surface codes applies physical CNOT between corresponding qubits in parallel.
questions.explanation: True. Transversal CNOT applies CNOT from qubit i of the control code to qubit i of the target code, for all i simultaneously. This preserves the code structure and implements logical CNOT, provided the codes have compatible orientations.
